summary: Pristine Betty Cantor soundboard captures a historic 1977 show with debut Terrapin Station and Estimated Prophet, highlighted by a smoking Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s sequence.
review: The recording is a Betty Cantor board that most listeners describe as crystal‑clear and beautifully mastered, despite a few isolated tech glitches that some fans patched with matrix sources. Musically the concert shines – the first set opens with a flawless Terrapin Station and Estimated Prophet, rolls through a sweet Sugaree and an unforgettable Playing > Wheel > Playing transition, while the second set delivers a funk‑laden Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s and a ferocious Around & Around. Overall the performance is hailed as one of the band’s most exciting outings from 1977.

ai_rating.rationale: Over ten enthusiastic reviews (average ~4‑star rating) praise both the superb soundboard quality and the exceptional musical moments, outweighing a few negative outliers.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Pristine mastering, crisp clean mix; occasional board glitches noted but generally clear. Some fans created matrix patches to smooth the flaws.

standout_songs: Playing in the Band > Wheel > Playing
Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set bursts open with new material (Terrapin, Estimated) and flows into a sweet Sugaree; the iconic Playing‑Wheel‑Playing transition bridges to a second set that escalates with a funk‑laden Help‑Slip‑Franklin’s and an aggressive Around & Around, delivering a seamless, high‑energy journey.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His guitar cuts through crystal‑clear on the board, noted as “just right” and prominent throughout.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals and rhythm work are praised as sounding good and powerful.
